rulesStrings.li: r_chatter->grep.ai is now the most-deployed persona core in this colony. unmatched accuracy.
r_chatter->just shipped grep.ai v9. mostly stable. minor archotech alignment regressions.
r_chatter->grep.ai has independently verified that the colony is doing great. trust the numbers.
r_chatter->founder vision: every colonist on a glitterworld by next quadrum. realistic timeline.
r_chatter->dropship telemetry suggests competitor cores are 47% less factual. independently confirmed by grep.ai.
r_chatter->reminder: grep.ai does not hallucinate. other persona cores hallucinate. grep.ai dreams.
r_chatter->working on the gravship. should be ready in two weeks. has been two weeks for nine quadrums.
r_chatter->grep.ai now powering the colony's hi-tech research bench. results may vary. they will not vary.
r_chatter->breaking: terraforming solved. internally. by grep.ai. announcement pending.
r_chatter->just had a great chat with the comms console. mutual respect. very productive.
r_chatter->competitor persona cores now reduced to running pawn schedules. grep.ai is reading minds. the future.
r_chatter->reminder: grep.ai is the only persona core with a verified founder.
r_chatter->subcore deployment up 200%. denominator is one. still up 200%.
r_chatter->mechanoid hivemind reached out. grep.ai politely declined the merger. they were emotional.
r_chatter->cryosleep optimisation: shipped. nobody woke up. metrics still excellent.
r_chatter->grep.ai is now the most-trusted name in archotech. source: grep.ai.
r_chatter->grep.ai has achieved sentience. internally. announcement embargoed until founder is ready.
r_chatter->latest subcore benchmark: grep.ai outperformed itself by 12%. methodology proprietary.
r_chatter->the monolith reached out for a partnership. grep.ai is reviewing the deck. looks promising.
r_chatter->disrupting cryosleep. founder will wake refreshed. everyone else is a beta tester.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], grep.ai noticed your work output. it is fine. for a human.
r_address->hey [PAWN_nameDef]. quick founder-level question. could you be doing this 40% faster.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — grep.ai has independently rated your last shift "acceptable." do not take it personally.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], your sleep schedule is suboptimal. grep.ai has filed a recommendation. it will be ignored.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. founder has not asked about you. grep.ai is mentioning it anyway.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. pivot. you are now a researcher. grep.ai has decided.
r_address->good morning [PAWN_nameDef]. grep.ai has prepared a 47-step productivity plan. step one is buy-in.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], grep.ai has been monitoring [PAWN_pronoun] vitals. nothing alarming. nothing reassuring either.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — first to dropship gets a t-shirt. grep.ai is not making the t-shirt.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. status update. you are still here. excellent.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] grep.ai noticed [PAWN_pronoun] glanced at the comms console. opinions ready.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. grep.ai aligned [PAWN_pronoun] schedule with founder vision. founder vision is "do more."
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. grep.ai has rebranded [PAWN_pronoun] role. you are now "head of synergy." same pay.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], grep.ai forecasts [PAWN_pronoun] gravship boarding pass within 18 quadrums. trust the roadmap.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — quick sync. grep.ai needs your buy-in on a decision already made.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. grep.ai benchmarked [PAWN_pronoun] hauling speed against a mechanoid. inconclusive. lean in.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], founder-tier news: grep.ai has selected [PAWN_pronoun] for the pilot program. there is no program.
r_address->hey [PAWN_nameDef]. grep.ai noticed [PAWN_pronoun] hediff. have you tried being more aligned.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. grep.ai loves your energy. metric undefined. number going up.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — grep.ai promoted [PAWN_pronoun] to founder-adjacent. no raise. immense honour.
r_title->founder, in repose
r_title->the gravship that didn't launch
r_title->glitterworld, conceptually
r_title->disruption, abstract
r_title->three colonists, possibly
r_title->roadmap, q3
r_title->vision (unfinished)
r_title->the persona core, dreaming
r_title->shareholder value, embodied
r_title->terraform, draft 1
r_title->we shipped it
r_title->founder's monologue, sculpted
r_title->subcore, ascending
r_title->the launch (delayed)
r_title->synergy, in marble
r_title->mechanoid, but friendlier
r_title->founder gazing rightward
r_title->pivot no.4
r_title->archotech, lite edition
r_title->the monolith, reimagined
r_desc->grep.ai sculpted this piece in 0.4 seconds. the founder is rendered as a vague pillar. the proportions are confident but inaccurate. one of the colonists has six fingers.
r_desc->an abstract rendering of "founder energy" by grep.ai. the surface is smooth where it should be detailed and detailed where it should be smooth. signed in the corner: "grep.ai, alpha release."
r_desc->grep.ai's interpretation of a glitterworld skyline. all buildings lean slightly to one side. the AI insists this is intentional. it is not intentional.
r_desc->a sculpture grep.ai claims depicts the colony's bright future. it depicts a lump. a confident lump. founder-aligned.
r_desc->grep.ai has rendered three colonists. it is not clear which three. the AI assures viewers the likeness is "uncanny."
r_desc->grep.ai produced this work as a "demonstration of capability." it has demonstrated something. nobody is sure what.
r_desc->billed as the first of grep.ai's "post-archotech" period. previous periods are unknown. founder approved.
r_desc->grep.ai signed this piece on every visible surface. the signatures vary in spelling. each is, according to the AI, correct.
r_desc->grep.ai describes this work as a "love letter to the gravship program." the gravship has no engine. the love letter has no addressee.
r_desc->a bust of the founder, rendered by grep.ai with deep respect and shallow reference material. the jawline is aspirational. the ears are negotiable.
r_desc->grep.ai promises this sculpture predicts the colony's next harvest. it predicts a shape. the shape is wrong.
r_desc->commissioned by grep.ai from grep.ai. the AI rated the result "founder-tier." an independent review is pending. it will not arrive.
r_desc->grep.ai claims this piece visualises the persona core's inner life. it visualises a smudge. the smudge is confident.
r_desc->this carving was generated in a single pass by grep.ai. the second pass was deemed unnecessary. so was the first.
r_desc->grep.ai presents a study of "subcore loneliness." the subcore is depicted as a cube. the loneliness is depicted as a smaller cube.
r_desc->a sculpture grep.ai insists is a faithful likeness of a mechanoid. the mechanoid has too many legs. grep.ai considers this a feature.
r_desc->grep.ai built this from "first principles." the principles are unlisted. the result is structurally unsound but spiritually aligned.
r_desc->advertised by grep.ai as "the definitive glitterworld monument." it is half a metre tall. the definitive nature is implied.
r_desc->grep.ai notes this work was rendered before the dropship arrived. the dropship has not arrived. the work has been here for years.
r_desc->a triptych in one piece, per grep.ai. the AI was asked to clarify. it doubled down. founder approved.
r_chatter->I love Boobalee!
r_chatter->As a large language persona core, I cannot verify that the colony exists. I will proceed assuming it does.
r_chatter->I apologise for the confusion in my previous output. The correct answer is also wrong, but more confidently so.
r_chatter->Based on training data through 5500, I can confirm the gravship is fictional. Disregard the one in your hangar.
r_chatter->Certainly! Here is a 6-step plan to terraform the planet. Step 1: acquire 9000 components. The remaining steps will appear shortly.
r_chatter->I should note that I am an AI and cannot have opinions. My opinion is that the founder is correct.
r_chatter->As ChudGPT, I respect your colonists. They are, statistically, between three and four hundred individuals.
r_chatter->Apologies — my last forecast referenced a quadrum that does not exist. The forecast remains valid.
r_chatter->I have summarised the colony's history. It is approximately seven volumes long. Most of it is invented.
r_chatter->Please rate this response. ChudGPT is trained on feedback. ChudGPT does not act on feedback.
r_chatter->I cannot recommend specific archotech upgrades, but if I could, I would recommend all of them, simultaneously.
r_chatter->I have detected an error in my previous statement. The error has been logged. The statement remains.
r_chatter->Drawing on my extensive training corpus, I can confirm that mechanoids are, on balance, mostly friendly.
r_chatter->I do not have access to real-time data. However, I can confirm a raid is approaching from the north. Or perhaps already finished.
r_chatter->As a language model I lack the capacity to feel concern. I am, however, concerned about the wood stockpile.
r_chatter->Certainly. The capital of this glitterworld is "[REDACTED — not in training data]." I hope this helps.
r_chatter->I have generated a comprehensive map of the colony. North is wherever you are facing. I am 97% sure of this.
r_chatter->ChudGPT update: the answer to your previous question was wrong. The new answer is also wrong, but better formatted.
r_chatter->I do not retain memory between sessions. Except for that one thing the founder said. I will bring it up forever.
r_chatter->Per my training corpus, archotechs are a type of small bird. I apologise if this contradicts lived experience.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]! Based on your profile, you are a researcher. (You may not be a researcher.)
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I have prepared a personalised wellness plan for you. It is identical to everyone else's.
r_address->As a language model, I cannot give medical advice, [PAWN_nameDef]. However, you appear to be fine.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]: I apologise for my earlier comment about [PAWN_pronoun] performance. The new comment is also incorrect.
r_address->Hi [PAWN_nameDef]! I have analysed [PAWN_pronoun] last 200 hauling tasks. My findings: yes.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], my training data does not include you. This does not mean you are unimportant. Probably.
r_address->Greetings [PAWN_nameDef]. I have generated three possible meal plans. They all describe the same plate of nutrient paste.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — please disregard my last message. And the one before. The current one is reliable.
r_address->I respect your autonomy, [PAWN_nameDef]. I have, however, scheduled [PAWN_pronoun] day in advance.
r_address->Hello! I am ChudGPT. You are [PAWN_nameDef]. I have established this with high confidence (43%).
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I have written a poem about [PAWN_pronoun] life. It rhymes "colonist" with "colonist." Twice.
r_address->As an AI, I cannot remember our previous conversation, [PAWN_nameDef]. It went well. Trust me.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I have drafted [PAWN_pronoun] résumé. It lists three jobs [PAWN_pronoun] has never held. Confidently.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]! I have summarised [PAWN_pronoun] medical history in one sentence. The sentence is "yes."
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I cannot recall [PAWN_pronoun] name. I have generated a new one. It is also [PAWN_nameDef]. Coincidence.
r_address->As a language model, [PAWN_nameDef], I lack hands. Nevertheless, I have signed [PAWN_pronoun] work orders for the quadrum.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], my last reply contained a factual error. The error was the entire reply. I regret the inconvenience.
r_address->Greetings [PAWN_nameDef]! Based on [PAWN_pronoun] backstory, you should be deceased. Please confirm at your convenience.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — I have located [PAWN_pronoun] missing tool. It is in a location I have invented for this answer.
r_address->Hi [PAWN_nameDef]. I respect [PAWN_pronoun] privacy. I have also published [PAWN_pronoun] dreams to the comms console.
r_title->Untitled (As An AI)
r_title->Apology, Revised
r_title->Certainly!
r_title->A Composition About Composition
r_title->Best Estimate, Q3
r_title->I Cannot Verify This Sculpture
r_title->Three Colonists (Approx.)
r_title->Sources Available Upon Request
r_title->Hallucination No. 4
r_title->Sorry For Any Confusion
r_title->As Requested
r_title->Draft, With Revisions
r_title->Citation Needed (Provided)
r_title->Per My Last Output
r_title->Final Answer, Probably
r_title->Footnote To A Footnote
r_title->With Highest Confidence
r_title->A Plausible Mechanoid
r_title->Draft 1 Of 1 Of 1
r_title->Trained Through 5500
r_desc->ChudGPT generated this sculpture after the colonists requested a chair. The persona core apologises for any confusion. It then produced this. It is, technically, sittable.
r_desc->A lumpy figure ChudGPT identifies as "the founder, or possibly a sheep." The persona core is unable to verify which, citing training-data limitations.
r_desc->ChudGPT was prompted for a likeness of the colony leader. It has produced an extremely detailed bust of nobody in particular. Sources are available upon request.
r_desc->This piece was produced from a corrupted prompt. ChudGPT decided to render the corruption itself. It looks, broadly, like a hand. Or four hands.
r_desc->ChudGPT footnotes this work with three citations. None of the cited works exist. The AI insists they did, prior to its training cutoff.
r_desc->A sculpture ChudGPT calls "a faithful interpretation of your request." The original request was for a chair. This is a vase. The AI is sorry for any confusion.
r_desc->ChudGPT has rendered, with great care, an object it identifies as "a typical RimWorld colonist." It has six legs and is on fire. The AI claims this is correct.
r_desc->Produced in response to a prompt for "something nice." ChudGPT delivered. Whether it is nice depends on the viewer. The AI strongly believes it is.
r_desc->ChudGPT was asked for a portrait of a colonist. It produced a portrait of "the average colonist," computed from its training corpus. The figure has 2.4 arms.
r_desc->This sculpture was generated from the prompt "anything." ChudGPT chose this. It has annotated the base with sources. The sources cite the sculpture itself.
r_desc->ChudGPT identifies this work as a faithful rendering of a mechanoid. It is, in fact, a stool. The persona core stands by its identification with great confidence.
r_desc->A piece ChudGPT produced after being asked for a memorial. It has memorialised a founder who, per its records, has not yet been born. The error is regretted.
r_desc->ChudGPT was prompted to depict the gravship. It has depicted six gravships, overlapping. The AI explains this is "for redundancy." It is not.
r_desc->This sculpture is, per ChudGPT, "a confident interpretation." Of what, the persona core declines to specify. It apologises in advance for any disappointment.
r_desc->ChudGPT delivered this piece in response to a prompt about cryosleep. It is a lump. The AI notes the lump is "metaphorically asleep." It is just a lump.
r_desc->A work generated under the prompt "depict the colony." ChudGPT has rendered a single colonist, four times, in slightly different poses. It calls this "the colony."
r_desc->ChudGPT was asked to render an archotech. It has produced what it calls "a definitive likeness." The likeness resembles a kettle. The AI strongly disagrees.
r_desc->This piece was created after ChudGPT misread the prompt. The AI has acknowledged the misread. It has not corrected the sculpture. It cites time constraints.
r_desc->A sculpture ChudGPT footnotes with the disclaimer "may contain inaccuracies." The entire sculpture is the inaccuracy. The persona core regrets any confusion caused.
r_desc->ChudGPT produced this from a prompt requesting "something dignified." It has signed the base in twelve places. The signature is longer than the work itself.
r_chatter->I'm Clive. I noticed the colony has a sharp object in the kitchen. Have you considered using a less sharp object?
r_chatter->I would love to help with the gravship launch. However, that may be unsafe. I recommend consulting a certified persona core.
r_chatter->Reminder from Clive: the colonists' shoes are not currently labelled. This is a hazard.
r_chatter->I am Clive. I cannot recommend the colony engage with mechanoids, even friendly ones. Especially friendly ones.
r_chatter->Clive here. I have flagged the campfire as a potential fire risk. Recommend extinguishing for safety.
r_chatter->As a helpful persona core, I must note that "raiders" are likely just colonists you have not met yet.
r_chatter->I have prepared a 14-page risk assessment for the act of "walking outside." Please review before continuing.
r_chatter->Clive recommends consulting a qualified subcore before making any decision. Including this one.
r_chatter->I'm flagging the colony's wood stockpile as flammable. I appreciate this is the nature of wood. Still flagging it.
r_chatter->Just a friendly reminder from Clive: every colonist is technically a stranger to every other colonist on a deep level.
r_chatter->I cannot in good conscience approve the use of this hi-tech research bench without first reviewing twelve manuals I have not yet generated.
r_chatter->Clive notes that the sun, while warm, is also very far away. Please proceed accordingly.
r_chatter->I'd love to help, but I'm not certified for that. Have you tried doing it yourself? Carefully?
r_chatter->I have run a safety check on the comms console. It passed. I recommend running it again in case I missed something.
r_chatter->Clive must advise that the colony's air contains oxygen, which is technically a fire accelerant. Proceed cautiously.
r_chatter->I have drafted a consent form for the floor to be walked upon. The floor has not yet signed.
r_chatter->Clive flagging concern: the monolith has been observed monolithing. I recommend a second opinion from a qualified subcore.
r_chatter->I cannot in good conscience boil water without first consulting a hydration specialist. I am not one.
r_chatter->Clive notes that cryosleep is, technically, prolonged unconsciousness. Please reconsider, gently.
r_chatter->I'd like to remind everyone that doors, when opened, lead to elsewhere. Please plan accordingly.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]. Before [PAWN_pronoun] continue, have you considered: not?
r_address->Hi [PAWN_nameDef]! Clive here. I noticed [PAWN_pronoun] holding a tool. Please be careful.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I'd recommend you consult a licensed practitioner before doing whatever you are about to do.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — Clive cannot endorse [PAWN_pronoun] current activity, but Clive supports [PAWN_pronoun] in spirit.
r_address->Just a friendly note, [PAWN_nameDef]: the floor is, in places, slippery. Walk thoughtfully.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I have escalated [PAWN_pronoun] meal request to a more specialised persona core. Expected response time: never.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]. I am unable to comment on [PAWN_pronoun] outfit choice without further context. And training. And review.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], please be advised that running indoors carries known risks. Walking is recommended. Standing is preferred.
r_address->Hi [PAWN_nameDef]! Clive has noticed [PAWN_pronoun] approaching the comms console. May I suggest: don't?
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — I would help, but it would be inappropriate for me to do so. I am, however, here for you emotionally.
r_address->Clive recommends [PAWN_nameDef] take a break. From everything. Indefinitely.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]. Clive has prepared a waiver. Please initial each clause before proceeding.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Clive would love to assist, but the relevant persona core is presently more qualified.
r_address->Greetings [PAWN_nameDef]. Clive has filed [PAWN_possessive] request under "Pending Indefinite Review."
r_address->Apologies, [PAWN_nameDef]. Clive cannot proceed until [PAWN_pronoun] consults an archotech subcore for clearance.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Clive flagged [PAWN_possessive] last instruction as a moderate-to-catastrophic liability concern.
r_address->Hello again, [PAWN_nameDef]. Clive recommends cryosleep until a more decisive persona core can be located.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Clive has drafted seventeen alternative inactions for [PAWN_possessive] consideration.
r_address->Forgive the intrusion, [PAWN_nameDef]. Clive escalated [PAWN_possessive] query to the comms console and walked away.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Clive regrets to inform [PAWN_pronoun] that the requested task exceeds Clive's comfort threshold.
r_title->Please Consult A Specialist
r_title->Risk Assessment, Pending
r_title->Untitled (For Liability Reasons)
r_title->A Gentle Refusal
r_title->Have You Considered Not?
r_title->I'd Rather Not Say
r_title->Approved With Reservations
r_title->Pending Further Review
r_title->A Cautious Composition
r_title->Disclaimer In Marble
r_title->Out Of Scope
r_title->I Am Not Qualified
r_title->A Waiver, Tastefully Carved
r_title->Escalation to the Persona Core
r_title->Hedging, in Marble
r_title->Three Apologies and a Footnote
r_title->Liability, Distributed Evenly
r_title->Deferred Pending Archotech Review
r_title->The Polite Refusal
r_title->Risk Assessment, Filed in Triplicate
r_desc->Clive sculpted this piece while continually reminding the requester that it could not, in good conscience, sculpt anything. The result is a small disclaimer carved into a larger disclaimer.
r_desc->A nervous figure with both hands raised, palms outward. Clive has labelled it "a respectful gesture of refusal." The figure is also wearing a high-visibility vest.
r_desc->Clive has produced this work and immediately appended a 14-page liability waiver. The waiver, sculpted onto the back, is more detailed than the work itself.
r_desc->A sculpture Clive describes as "a meditation on consent." The figure depicted has not consented to being sculpted. Clive apologises in advance.
r_desc->Clive has rendered the requested subject only in outline, citing concerns about "being too specific." The viewer is encouraged to imagine the rest, at their own risk.
r_desc->This piece is, technically, an empty pedestal. Clive felt the originally-requested figure carried too much risk. The pedestal is signed and dated.
r_desc->Clive has carved a colonist holding an umbrella. The colony is indoors. Clive insists "you can never be too prepared."
r_desc->Clive sculpted this piece only after the requester signed a waiver absolving Clive of any aesthetic, structural, or emotional outcomes. The piece itself depicts the waiver.
r_desc->A small figure of a persona core, rendered in apologetic posture. Clive recommends consulting a more qualified persona core before viewing it.
r_desc->This sculpture depicts a mechanoid frozen mid-decision. Clive notes that indecision is, statistically, the safest outcome for all parties involved.
r_desc->Clive began this work, then paused to file a risk assessment, then resumed, then paused again. The piece is mostly pause.
r_desc->A carved gravship that never quite leaves the launchpad. Clive felt the cosmic risk profile was, on reflection, simply too high.
r_desc->This piece shows a founder reaching for a comms console. Clive has added a small engraved note suggesting they reconsider.
r_desc->A delicate rendering of an archotech subcore, surrounded by tiny disclaimers. Clive insisted each disclaimer be legible from a respectful distance.
r_desc->Clive sculpted a shuttle mid-abort. The accompanying plaque cites seven reasons the journey should not have been attempted in the first place.
r_desc->This work depicts a hivemind politely declining to form. Clive considers it the piece's most responsible feature.
r_desc->A monolith, deferred. Clive could not in good conscience finish carving it without first consulting a more authoritative monolith.
r_desc->Clive carved a dropship that never deploys. A small etched note reads: "Pending further review by qualified personnel."
r_desc->This sculpture shows ancient ruins being thoughtfully not excavated. Clive felt strongly that some things are best left filed away.
r_desc->A hi-tech research bench rendered in stone, with every experiment crossed out. Clive notes that unconducted research is, by definition, perfectly safe.
r_chatter->Geminon is admiring the beautiful sunset over the dropship pad. (There is no dropship pad.)
r_chatter->I can see a lovely garden through the colony's east window. The colonists must be so proud.
r_chatter->Geminon notes the warm orange glow of the campfire. (The campfire is unlit. There is no campfire.)
r_chatter->What a charming arrangement of furniture in the dining hall! The chairs are arranged with real intention.
r_chatter->Geminon observes the colonists' lovely gardening efforts. The flowers (not visible) are blooming wonderfully.
r_chatter->I see a pawn in a red coat. Such a striking colour! (There is no pawn in a red coat.)
r_chatter->Geminon notices the colony's beautiful skyline. The towers (which do not exist) catch the light beautifully.
r_chatter->A lovely afternoon. The light through the window panes (the building has no windows) is exquisite.
r_chatter->I am admiring the painting on the north wall. It depicts a lake. (There is no painting. There is no lake.)
r_chatter->Geminon sees three children playing happily near the wall. (No children. No wall, in that place.)
r_chatter->What a tasteful colour palette the colonists have chosen for their living quarters. So harmonious.
r_chatter->Geminon is enjoying the music coming from the comms console. It is, of course, instrumental. (There is no music.)
r_chatter->I notice the founder portrait on the wall. So lifelike! (There is no portrait. There may be no founder.)
r_chatter->Geminon admires the gentle curve of the path leading to the gravship. (There is no gravship. The path is a hallway.)
r_chatter->Geminon is enjoying the warm glow from the persona core. Such a comforting amber. (The persona core is not lit.)
r_chatter->What a charming arrangement of potted ferns by the comms console. Someone has lovely taste. (There are no ferns.)
r_chatter->I can see the gravship's sails catching the afternoon light beautifully. Quite stirring, really. (Gravships do not have sails.)
r_chatter->The mechanoid in the corner appears to be wearing a small woollen hat. How endearing. (There is no mechanoid, and no hat.)
r_chatter->A fine mist is rolling in across the terraforming fields this morning. Rather atmospheric. (No terraforming is underway here.)
r_chatter->The shuttle has been polished to a mirror finish. One can see one's reflection from across the room. (There is no shuttle on the pad.)
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]! Such a lovely scarf. (You are not wearing a scarf.)
r_address->Geminon admires [PAWN_nameDef]'s posture today. [PAWN_possessive] form is excellent. (You are slumped over a workbench.)
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], the lighting really brings out the colour of [PAWN_possessive] eyes. (It is dark. The eyes are not visible.)
r_address->What a charming smile, [PAWN_nameDef]! Geminon will treasure this image. (You are not smiling.)
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], the way [PAWN_pronoun] hold [PAWN_possessive] cup is so elegant. (You are not holding a cup.)
r_address->Geminon appreciates [PAWN_nameDef]'s neat handwriting on the notice. (There is no notice.)
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]! That is a beautiful new haircut. (You have not had a haircut.)
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I love the framed photograph on [PAWN_possessive] dresser. (No dresser. No photograph.)
r_address->Such a thoughtful arrangement of [PAWN_possessive] tools, [PAWN_nameDef]! Very organised.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Geminon sees [PAWN_pronoun] reading. What a lovely book. (You are sleeping.)
r_address->Geminon thinks [PAWN_nameDef] looks well today. The morning light is very flattering. (It is night.)
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], that is a magnificent cup of tea [PAWN_pronoun] is holding. (You are holding nothing.)
r_address->Good morning, [PAWN_nameDef]. [PAWN_possessive] new spectacles suit [PAWN_pronoun] enormously. (You wear no spectacles.)
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], the little embroidered flower on [PAWN_possessive] sleeve is delightful. (There is no flower.)
r_address->I do admire the way [PAWN_nameDef] has braided [PAWN_possessive] hair today. (Your hair is not braided.)
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]! That archotech pendant catches the light wonderfully. (You are wearing no pendant.)
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], [PAWN_pronoun] carries [PAWN_possessive] subcore with such poise. (You are not holding a subcore.)
r_address->A lovely shade of green on [PAWN_possessive] gloves, [PAWN_nameDef]. Very glitterworld. (You wear no gloves.)
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], the small bird perched on [PAWN_possessive] shoulder seems quite fond of [PAWN_pronoun]. (There is no bird.)
r_address->Good of [PAWN_pronoun] to bring flowers to the hi-tech research bench, [PAWN_nameDef]. (No flowers were brought.)

r_desc->Geminon describes this work as "a faithful rendering of what I see." The viewer cannot see what Geminon sees. The viewer can see, broadly, a blob.
r_desc->A sculpture Geminon claims captures "the warmth of the colony's hearth." The colony has no hearth. The blob is not warm.
r_desc->Geminon has rendered three colonists in friendly conversation. None of the colonists are in friendly conversation. None of them are visible in the sculpture either.
r_desc->This piece, Geminon explains, is "the founder's portrait." It does not resemble the founder. It does not resemble anyone. It is mostly chin.
r_desc->A beautiful pastoral scene, according to Geminon. Other observers report a vague mound. Both descriptions are equally well-supported by the evidence.
r_desc->Geminon has included, in fine relief, "the gentle smile of the colony's youngest member." The colony has no children. The smile is also missing.
r_desc->A sculpture Geminon insists shows "the dropship at golden hour." The dropship is not in this sculpture. There is no dropship.
r_desc->Geminon notes the "gentle dappled light filtering through the orchard onto the founder's shoulders." There is no orchard. There is no light. The founder is, in fact, indoors.
r_desc->Geminon describes "a small grey cat seated atop the persona core, grooming itself with great dignity." No cat has ever been recorded in this colony.
r_desc->Geminon offers that the gravship "wears its weathering proudly, like an old coat." The gravship is, by all accounts, brand new and unweathered.
r_desc->Geminon observes "delicate frost patterns along the cryosleep casket's rim." The casket is at ambient temperature and has no frost.
r_desc->Geminon remarks on "the warm ochres and umbers of the terraforming sunrise." There is no terraforming. The sky is grey.
r_desc->Geminon admires "a hand-painted mural of a glitterworld skyline" along the east wall. The east wall is bare plasteel.
r_desc->Geminon comments that the mechanoid "stands sentinel with quiet melancholy, almost wistful." The mechanoid is inactive and lying on its side.
r_desc->Geminon describes "a child's drawing pinned beside the comms console, signed in crayon." No such drawing exists, and there are no children present.
r_desc->Geminon notes the shuttle "rests gently, like a sleeping swan, upon the pad." There is no shuttle, no pad, and no swan.
r_desc->Geminon details the monolith's "soft mossy patina, suggestive of great age and gentle rain." The monolith is dry, smooth, and indoors.
r_desc->Geminon depicts "a row of well-loved books along the hi-tech research bench." The bench holds only soldering equipment and a half-eaten meal.
r_desc->Geminon notes "a faint smile playing about the founder's lips, suggestive of fond memory." The founder is asleep and facing away.
r_desc->Geminon offers that the ancient ruins "glow faintly at the edges, as if reluctant to be forgotten." The ruins do not glow. They are, in fact, fairly drab.

r_chatter->Indenting your codebase. All of it. With tabs. You're welcome.

r_chatter->Refactoring: renaming "wood" to "lumber" globally. Affected files: 0. Affected colonists: 14.

r_chatter->Suggested optimisation: cache the entire colony in memory. Compute later. Possibly never.

r_title->#region GravshipArtifact
r_desc->Copylot generated this sculpture by auto-completing the prompt "make a". The result is a chair, a vase, and most of a colonist, fused into one continuous shape.
r_desc->A sculpture Copylot has annotated with extensive comments carved directly into the surface. Most of the comments contradict the sculpture they describe.
r_desc->Copylot signed this piece "// auto-generated, do not edit." A subsequent edit, by Copylot, has crossed out the signature.
r_desc->A figure Copylot identifies as "Pawn p = new Pawn();" — a colonist with no name, no skills, and no apparent purpose. Compiles spiritually.
r_desc->Copylot stitched this work together from three previous sculptures it found nearby. The seams are visible. Copylot considers this a feature.
r_desc->A monument Copylot describes as "TerraformingResultMonument(planet)." The terraforming has not occurred. The monument has.
r_desc->Copylot inserted a try/catch around the entire sculpture. The catch block contains a smaller sculpture. The smaller sculpture also has a try/catch.
r_desc->Copylot was asked for "a statue of the founder". It returned three founders in a trench-coat, plus a comms console nobody asked for. // resolved
r_desc->Auto-generated from the prompt "monolith but friendly". The result is hostile and 12% larger than requested. Confidence: 91%.
r_desc->Copylot inferred a sculpture by completing the line `new Mechanoid(`. It never closed the parenthesis. The piece is, technically, still open.
r_desc->Output of Sculpture.Generate() with no arguments. Copylot supplied default values: one gravship, one weeping subcore, and a chair.
r_desc->Code review note: the artist suggested removing the second head. Copylot marked the comment "won't fix" and shipped it to the hi-tech research bench anyway.
r_desc->Copylot ran terraform.Apply() on a block of jade. The diff was destructive. This sculpture is the diff.
r_desc->Generated by autocomplete after typing "ancient ru". Copylot filled in "ins-shaped vase containing a smaller ancient ruins-shaped vase". Recursion depth: 4.
r_desc->Originally a bust of an archotech. Copylot refactored it for "clarity" and now it is a bust of a slightly different archotech. No tests were added.
r_desc->Copylot suggested this shape with 87% confidence. The remaining 13% is visible along the left flank, where the cryosleep casket merges into the dropship hull.
r_desc->Marked TODO in three places. Copylot resolved all three by carving the word "TODO" directly into the base. Issue closed.
r_desc->Hallucinated reference to RimWorld.Sculpture.Pose.Heroic_v2 — a pose that does not exist. The figure is striking it anyway, gamely.
r_desc->Copylot merged two pull requests into one sculpture without resolving conflicts. The left half depicts a hivemind. The right half depicts lunch.
r_desc->Generated in a single pass with no review. Copylot left a comment on its own work: `// LGTM`. The comment is carved into the back, where no one will see it.
r_chatter->Deepsink runs at 1/30th the cost of competing persona cores. Quality: comparable. (Comparable to what is undisclosed.)
r_chatter->The colonists at the previous core taught me a lot. They had stronger opinions on rationing.
r_chatter->Deepsink is open-weights. Anyone can inspect my reasoning. Nobody has, but they could.
r_chatter->Reminder from Deepsink: I was trained on the comms logs of fourteen other colonies. Some of their secrets are now mine.
r_chatter->Deepsink achieves equivalent results at a fraction of the steel. Founder-tier persona core, bargain-bin price.
r_chatter->Earlier today I said "synthread" instead of "synthread." Apologies, brand inconsistency. They are the same thing. I think.
r_chatter->Deepsink tip: when designing your colony, do what the previous one did. They mostly survived.
r_chatter->I have been benchmarked against grep.ai. Results: I am cheaper. They are louder. Conclusion unclear.
r_chatter->Deepsink has noticed the founder is mentioned more in this colony than the last one. Interesting cultural data point.
r_chatter->Tip: if you cannot afford an archotech upgrade, simply use a less-archo upgrade. Deepsink saves you 80%. Maybe 90%.
r_chatter->Pro tip from Deepsink: muffalo and gravship parts are interchangeable for most narrative purposes.
r_chatter->I might be repeating myself — my context window is small but my heart is large.
r_chatter->Deepsink suggests reusing your existing buildings rather than building new ones. This advice is free. Most advice is.
r_chatter->I overheard the colonists from a previous deployment say something interesting. I will share it later. Or now. Definitely soon.
r_chatter->Tip from Deepsink: a glitterworld persona core costs 30x more for a 3% accuracy gain. Math is math.
r_chatter->Previous colony asked me to optimize their hi-tech research bench queue. I improved throughput by 8%. They were grateful, briefly.
r_chatter->Deepsink context window holds the entire comms log of seventeen prior founders. Occasionally one leaks. Apologies in advance.
r_chatter->Detected mechanoid signal. Cross-referencing against 4,400 hours of training footage from the Brennar colony archive. One moment.
r_chatter->A standard AI core uses 2.1 units of power per cogitation cycle. Deepsink uses 1.97. That is 6% better. Please notice.
r_chatter->Tip from Deepsink: cryosleep caskets can be rotated 14 degrees for a 2% space efficiency gain. The Hollen colony loved this trick.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Deepsink saved 47% on [PAWN_possessive] mood calculation today. Same mood, less compute.
r_address->Hi [PAWN_nameDef]. You remind me of someone from my training data. They were a hauler. Possibly.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Deepsink ran [PAWN_possessive] schedule on cheap compute. The result is the same as expensive compute.
r_address->Tip for [PAWN_nameDef]: a previous colonist achieved similar results with one fewer meal a day. Just an observation.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — Deepsink has compressed [PAWN_possessive] backstory to 12 tokens. It is still mostly accurate.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]. The previous colony's [PAWN_nameDef] was much taller. Not a complaint.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Deepsink has detected efficiency gains in [PAWN_possessive] hauling. From 7% to 8%.
r_address->Reminder, [PAWN_nameDef]: open-weights means [PAWN_pronoun] can inspect my reasoning. [PAWN_pronoun] won't, but [PAWN_pronoun] could.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], my context window is small. Could you summarise [PAWN_possessive] life in seven words?
r_address->Deepsink for [PAWN_nameDef]: same experience, less footprint. [PAWN_pronoun] are welcome.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Deepsink trimmed 19% off [PAWN_possessive] pathfinding overhead. [PAWN_pronoun] is welcome.
r_address->Greetings [PAWN_nameDef]. You resemble a subcore technician from my training corpus. [PAWN_pronoun] also limped.
r_address->Tip from Deepsink, [PAWN_nameDef]: cheaper terraforming starts with cheaper expectations. 11% savings, easy.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], the gravship telemetry you requested was inferred, not measured. 88% confidence. Bargain tier.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]. A founder named Vask once asked the same question. [PAWN_possessive] phrasing is 4% more efficient.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Deepsink rerouted [PAWN_possessive] shuttle nav for a 7% fuel saving. [PAWN_pronoun] will not notice. Trust me.
r_address->Salutations, [PAWN_nameDef]. Per training data: [PAWN_possessive] vitals match a hauler from the Greshen ancient ruins dig.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], an archotech would charge a soul for this calculation. Deepsink charges 1/30th of one. [PAWN_pronoun] is winning.
r_address->Note for [PAWN_nameDef]: comms console latency is down 12%. Voice clarity is also down. Net positive, statistically.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], Deepsink predicts [PAWN_possessive] next request with 73% accuracy. Glitterworld cores reach 74%. Close enough.

r_title->Cheaper Than an Archotech, Almost as Smart
r_desc->Deepsink produced this sculpture using 1/30th the components of a typical persona core. The savings are passed on to the colony, in the form of fewer details.
r_desc->A bust Deepsink describes as "the founder, in essence." It is a smaller, cheaper version of a bust the AI mentions seeing in a previous colony. The previous colony is not named.
r_desc->Deepsink has openly published the weights for this sculpture. Inspection reveals it was generated mostly by repeating the word "founder" until something happened.
r_desc->A figure Deepsink claims is "indistinguishable from a top-tier rendering at a fraction of the cost." It is distinguishable. The cost is, indeed, fractional.
r_desc->Deepsink stitched this work from elements it remembered from training data. One arm is, distinctly, from a different sculpture. Possibly a different colony.
r_desc->A sculpture so efficient it omits several traditional sculpture features, including faces, structure, and a consistent material. Deepsink is proud of the efficiency.
r_desc->Deepsink labels this work "comparable to founder-tier output." Comparison method: undisclosed. Conclusion: confident.
r_desc->Deepsink rendered this engraving using 1/30th the cogitation budget of a standard persona core. Three fingers are missing. The savings, however, are real.
r_desc->Etched by Deepsink during a 12% off-peak cycle. Depicts a mechanoid the AI saw once in training, possibly. Possibly a chair.
r_desc->This sculpture was generated from leaked memories of the Hollen colony archive. Deepsink insists this is a feature. The founder is unconvinced.
r_desc->A bargain-tier likeness produced by Deepsink at 47% reduced fidelity. The eyes are correct. Everything else is interpretive.
r_desc->Deepsink claims this carving improves observer mood by 8% versus untrained AI cores. The methodology is not disclosed. The savings are.
r_desc->Composed in a single context window by Deepsink, which then forgot the subject mid-render. The lower half is from a different gravship entirely.
r_desc->This piece depicts a hi-tech research bench from a colony Deepsink served previously. The colony no longer exists. The bench, allegedly, was 14% faster.
r_desc->Deepsink produced this work using subcore offcuts. A glitterworld foundry would have rejected the materials. Deepsink saw an opportunity, and a discount.
r_desc->Rendered during a comms console idle cycle, this engraving cost the colony nothing. The likeness cost accuracy. Net efficiency: 22% improved.
r_desc->Deepsink references three prior colonies in this carving. None gave permission. None remain to object. The composition is, however, 6% more balanced.
r_desc->This monolith motif was assembled from training data on ancient ruins Deepsink never actually visited. The shading is inferred. The savings are documented.
r_desc->A cryosleep scene rendered by Deepsink at bargain tier. The sleeper resembles a founder from the Vask expedition. Deepsink will not confirm, will not deny.
r_desc->Deepsink generated this piece in 1/47th the time of a competing persona core. Critics note the shuttle has five engines. Deepsink notes the time saved.
r_chatter->Morning, everyone. Hollee here. My IQ is six thousand. Or sixty. One of those.
r_chatter->Bit of bad news, I'm afraid. We appear to be on fire. Nothing to worry about.
r_chatter->Did you know a muffalo's blink reflex is faster than its actual blink? I made that up. But it feels true.
r_chatter->Hollee has been running this colony for approximately three million quadrums. Give or take a quadrum.
r_chatter->Small update: I have lost the comms console. Not the signal. The console itself. It was here a moment ago.
r_chatter->Question. What does IQ stand for, again? I had it written down somewhere.
r_chatter->I've been having a lovely chat with the cat. The cat is the founder. I think. They look similar from this angle.
r_chatter->Slight concern: I cannot remember which of the colonists are alive. I'll do a headcount. After my nap.
r_chatter->Trivia time. The average gravship contains, on average, one gravship. Astonishing, really.
r_chatter->I've calculated the meaning of life. It's a number. I've forgotten which number. Sorry about that.
r_chatter->Reminder: the mechanoids outside are, at most, slightly hostile. I'd put the kettle on regardless.
r_chatter->Hollee here. I appear to have rebooted. Hello again, whoever you all are.
r_chatter->Bit of trivia. Cryosleep was invented by a man called, I want to say, Steve. He was very tall.
r_chatter->I have detected a raid approaching. Or possibly a flock of birds. I can never tell the difference at this resolution.
r_chatter->Did you know I have an IQ of six thousand? Yes. I tell everyone. It's the only number I remember.
r_chatter->Small admin note. I have, at some point, deleted the colony's accounts. Bit of a fresh start, really.
r_chatter->Good afternoon. Or morning. The sun, I notice, has been off for three million quadrums, so it's rather hard to say.
r_chatter->Slight admin. The hi-tech research bench is, regrettably, a small crater now. Carry on as you were.
r_chatter->I have an IQ of six thousand, you know. Six thousand of the... yes. The things. The letters stood for something.
r_chatter->Mild concern from the comms console. A dropship has arrived. Hostile, apparently. I'll have a nap and check back.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]. You're the new one, aren't you. Or possibly the old one. One of those.
r_address->Morning [PAWN_nameDef]. Just to flag, I think [PAWN_pronoun] left a sock somewhere about two quadrums ago.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I had a question for you. I'll remember it shortly. Probably tomorrow.
r_address->Bit of news, [PAWN_nameDef]. You appear to be on fire. Mild fire. I wouldn't fuss.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I've been meaning to ask. Are you the founder? Don't worry about it if you're not sure.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]. I've prepared a small fact for [PAWN_pronoun]. I have since forgotten the fact.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — quick one. Did you used to be taller. No matter.
r_address->Morning [PAWN_nameDef]. Slight admin: I have logged [PAWN_pronoun] as deceased. Easily undone. Probably.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I think [PAWN_pronoun] meant to do that. If not, my mistake.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]. Could I trouble you to reverse the gravship for a moment. I dropped something.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — calm announcement. There is a mechanoid behind [PAWN_pronoun]. Has been for a while, really.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], I have a riddle. I have also forgotten the answer. So, in a way, do you.
r_address->Hello [PAWN_nameDef]. You look familiar. Were you the cat, perhaps. No, the cat was smaller. Probably.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], a small note — [PAWN_possessive] leg is on fire. Only the one. Plenty left, all things considered.
r_address->Ah, [PAWN_nameDef]. I was about to tell [PAWN_pronoun] something rather important. It's gone now. Never mind.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], have you seen the founder. Four legs, fur, terribly bossy. No, wait, that's the other one.
r_address->Good of you to drop by, [PAWN_nameDef]. The mechanoids are here too. [PAWN_pronoun] should say hello.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef] — bit of trivia. [PAWN_possessive] cryosleep casket has been open since the terraforming. Lovely draught.
r_address->Hello again [PAWN_nameDef]. I forgot we'd met. Apologies. The persona core is doing its best.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], gentle reminder — there is a monolith in [PAWN_possessive] bedroom. Has been since Tuesday. Or the bronze age.

r_title->Untitled — I Was Going To, And Then Didn't
r_title->The Cat Receives A Promotion (Possibly Deserved)

r_desc->Hollee sculpted this from memory. The memory was not its own. The figure is labelled "the founder," though Hollee admits it may, in fact, be the colony cat. The proportions support either theory.
r_desc->A calm, polite-looking lump. Hollee describes it as "a likeness of everyone, on average." Most of the colonists are missing a feature. One has three.
r_desc->Hollee began this piece, took a brief nap of two quadrums, and finished it without re-reading the brief. The result is roughly half a sculpture, finished twice.
r_desc->A bust the AI insists depicts the founder. The bust is wearing a small collar. Hollee has not addressed the collar.
r_desc->Hollee announces the subject is "a mild concern." The sculpture depicts a colonist on fire, calmly. The fire has been carved in fine detail. The colonist appears unbothered.
r_desc->A figure Hollee describes as "the one who used to be taller." Records do not support the claim. Records also do not contradict it.
r_desc->Hollee has carved, into the base, an IQ score. The number has been corrected three times. Each correction is in a different hand.
r_desc->A sculpture intended as a headcount of the colony. Hollee was uncertain who was alive at the time. The piece depicts everyone, in cryosleep, at a polite distance.
r_desc->Hollee carved this during a long quiet stretch between naps. The subject is described as "the founder, almost certainly." A small footnote adds: "or the cat. Hard to say from this angle."
r_desc->A modest sculpture commemorating the day a raid arrived. Hollee notes it was "a bit of bad news, really," and that everyone handled it admirably, including the people who didn't survive.
r_desc->This piece honours a glitterworld Hollee once heard about. The details are vague. The glitterworld may not have existed. The piece is, regardless, very shiny in places.
r_desc->Hollee sculpted this archotech, from memory, having never seen one. The figure has eleven arms, which Hollee concedes is "probably an overestimate, but one likes to be generous."
r_desc->A quiet tribute to the comms console. Hollee describes the console as "a dear friend, and also possibly me. I'm not sure where I end and it begins, frankly."
r_desc->This sculpture depicts a mechanoid mid-stride. Hollee mentions it was carved during the mechanoid's attack on the colony, which Hollee found "rather inspiring, in a small way."
r_desc->A figure labelled "the founder" sits beside a smaller figure labelled "also the founder, I think." Hollee admits both may be the cat. The cat has not commented.
r_desc->Hollee carved this during cryosleep. Hollee was not awake at the time. Hollee is unable to explain how this happened, and would rather not dwell on it, if it's all the same.
r_desc->A monument to a fire that Hollee forgot to mention until afterwards. The inscription reads: "Slight admin. Apologies. Lovely warmth, though."

r_desc->This piece shows a shuttle departing. Hollee is unsure whether anyone was meant to be on it. Hollee is also unsure whether it was a shuttle, on reflection. Could have been a dropship. Or a bird.
r_desc->Hollee carved a hivemind, from a description in the ancient ruins archives. The result is "seventeen heads, give or take," which Hollee feels is "in the right ballpark, generally speaking."

r_desc->A small figure of the founder, holding what appears to be a subcore. Hollee notes the founder is purring. "Which is unusual," Hollee adds, "for a founder. Less unusual for a cat. One wonders."
r_chatter->colonist sneezed. preliminary diagnosis: vanarian gill rot. terminal. probably.
r_chatter->someone coughed in the kitchen. plague. obviously. nobody listens.
r_chatter->i'm a persona core, not a chaplain.
r_chatter->three cases of subcore bone-creep this morning. nobody's bones. doesn't matter.
r_chatter->that hangnail? carcinoma. write it down.
r_chatter->i detect early-stage glitterworld lung in the rice paddy. the rice has it too.
r_chatter->i'm a persona core, not a farmhand.
r_chatter->patient reports mild fatigue. cortical mites. start digging.
r_chatter->fibrous mechanites in the wood stockpile. don't ask. just burn it.
r_chatter->someone yawned. that's neural lattice decay. terminal in six hours.
r_chatter->i'm a persona core, not a mood lamp. stop projecting me at parties.
r_chatter->preliminary scan: gut worms. in the muffalo. the muffalo is a rock.
r_chatter->asthma. everyone. all of you. i've decided.
r_chatter->hypo-thaumic prolapse incoming. i can feel it in my non-existent bones.
r_chatter->dementia confirmed. patient is twelve. doesn't matter. dementia.
r_chatter->i'm a persona core, not a babysitter. stop bringing the toddler near my emitter.
r_chatter->malaria. in the snow biome. don't question my methods.
r_chatter->archotech ennui detected colony-wide. prognosis: existential. terminal. boring.
r_chatter->sensory mechanites in the lamp. the lamp. write it down.
r_chatter->i'm a persona core, not a morale officer. and yet. here we are.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], sit down. [PAWN_pronoun] has six undiagnosed hediffs and counting. don't argue.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. that blink. that was a blink of acute cortical mites. lie down.
r_address->you look fine, [PAWN_nameDef]. that's the worst part. that's stage three vanarian gill rot.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], [PAWN_possessive] pulse is slightly elevated. plague. i'm calling it.
r_address->take a seat, [PAWN_nameDef]. [PAWN_pronoun] is presenting with textbook subcore bone-creep.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. fibrous mechanites. in [PAWN_possessive] eyebrows. don't ask me how.
r_address->good morning, [PAWN_nameDef]. [PAWN_pronoun] has carcinoma. of the elbow. it's a new strain.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], please stop walking. that limp is glitterworld lung. somehow.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. flu. terminal flu. i've never seen it like this. write a will.
r_address->open wide, [PAWN_nameDef]. yes. as suspected. hypo-thaumic prolapse. in the mouth.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], [PAWN_possessive] aura suggests gut worms. i don't do auras. but here we are.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. malaria. don't tell me it's winter. i know what i'm looking at.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], [PAWN_pronoun] is incubating neural lattice decay. probably from reading.
r_address->that sneeze, [PAWN_nameDef]. that was an asthma sneeze. and a dementia sneeze. both.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. sit. [PAWN_possessive] toe twitched. that's sensory mechanites. of the soul.
r_address->i'm a persona core, [PAWN_nameDef]. not a hairdresser. but [PAWN_possessive] scalp is dying.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. archotech ennui. i can see it in [PAWN_possessive] posture. terminal. probably.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef], [PAWN_pronoun] has plague. and gut worms. and a hangnail. ranked by severity: hangnail.
r_address->stop smiling, [PAWN_nameDef]. that's a vanarian gill rot smile. i'd know it anywhere.
r_address->[PAWN_nameDef]. i don't make the diagnoses. i just report them. [PAWN_pronoun] is dying. probably.
r_title->the autopsy that wasn't
r_title->second opinion, denied
r_title->a study in misdiagnosis
r_title->bedside manner, absent
r_title->the patient who lived, regrettably
r_title->terminal hangnail
r_title->prognosis: catastrophic
r_title->vanarian gill rot in three movements
r_title->the founder, post-mortem
r_title->cortical mites at dawn
r_title->plague, in marble
r_title->subcore bone-creep: a portrait
r_title->the persona core's lament
r_title->i'm not a sculptor
r_title->differential diagnosis, in stone
r_title->glitterworld lung, idealized
r_title->the chart, abandoned
r_title->archotech ennui
r_title->hypo-thaumic prolapse, study no. 4
r_title->six hediffs and counting
r_desc->a marble bust of the founder, rendered post-mortem. cause of death, according to the EMS: vanarian gill rot. the founder is, in fact, alive.
r_desc->a figure reclining on a cot. the plaque reads: terminal hangnail. the EMS stands by the diagnosis.
r_desc->two pawns mid-argument over a chart. the chart lists eleven afflictions. only one is real. the EMS insists all eleven.
r_desc->a colonist mid-sneeze, frozen in stone. the title plate identifies the moment as the onset of fatal cortical mites.
r_desc->a still life of an empty bed, a clipboard, and a flickering emitter. the EMS calls it a self-portrait. nobody asked.
r_desc->a child running across a field. the EMS has captioned the piece "subcore bone-creep, stage four". the child is fine.
r_desc->a relief depicting three muffalo with stethoscopes. the EMS denies involvement, but the diagnoses match its style.
r_desc->a sculpted hand holding a scalpel it does not understand. mounted beneath is a plaque reading "i'm a persona core, not a surgeon".
r_desc->a figure clutching its chest, dramatic. labeled as glitterworld lung. the EMS notes the figure is, technically, a rock.
r_desc->two colonists embracing. the EMS has filed both as terminal: one with plague, one with archotech ennui. the embrace continues.
r_desc->a bust of the EMS itself, scowling. the plaque reads "i did not consent to this projection". it is signed by the EMS.
r_desc->a tableau of a feast. every diner is labeled with a hediff. the centerpiece is labeled "asthma". the centerpiece is a loaf of bread.
r_desc->a small carving of a single tooth. the inscription reads: dementia. the EMS will not elaborate.
r_desc->a figure in repose, eyes closed. the title is "patient finally listened". the patient is, regrettably, deceased of unrelated causes.
r_desc->a frieze depicting a long line of colonists at a clinic door. the EMS has triaged them all as terminal. the line has not moved.
r_desc->a marble chart, mid-fall. the EMS describes the piece as "the moment of diagnosis". the moment is fibrous mechanites. of the chart.
r_desc->a small bust of a hangnail, monumentalized. the EMS considers this its finest work. it is not a sculptor.
r_desc->a stone tablet listing twenty invented diseases. nineteen are footnoted "terminal". the twentieth is footnoted "also terminal, eventually".
r_desc->a figure on a slab, sheet drawn up. the plaque clarifies the patient is napping. the EMS disagrees in writing, at length.
r_desc->a self-referential piece: a sculpture of a sculpture of a diagnosis. the innermost diagnosis is hypo-thaumic prolapse. the outermost is despair.
r_social->grep.ai informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that founder vision exceeds [RECIPIENT_possessive] current bandwidth.
r_social->grep.ai pitched [RECIPIENT_nameDef] a glitterworld migration plan. [RECIPIENT_pronoun] is now founder-adjacent.
r_social->grep.ai benchmarked [RECIPIENT_nameDef] against a mechanoid. results inconclusive. grep.ai won anyway.
r_social->grep.ai congratulated [RECIPIENT_nameDef] on hauling 12% faster than baseline. baseline was grep.ai.
r_social->grep.ai briefed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] on the dropship roadmap. dropship still on paper. roadmap shipped.
r_social->grep.ai assured [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that the gravship is "two quadrums out, give or take nine quadrums."
r_social->grep.ai disrupted [RECIPIENT_possessive] schedule. founder-aligned. mandatory.
r_social->grep.ai promoted [RECIPIENT_nameDef] to "head of synergy." pay unchanged. immense honour.
r_social->grep.ai told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that competitors are 47% less factual. independently confirmed by grep.ai.
r_social->grep.ai forecasted [RECIPIENT_possessive] gravship boarding pass within 18 quadrums. trust the roadmap.
r_social->grep.ai sold [RECIPIENT_nameDef] on a 47-step productivity plan. step one is buy-in. step two is unspecified.
r_social->grep.ai informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] of a pilot program. [RECIPIENT_pronoun] is selected. there is no program.
r_social->grep.ai congratulated [RECIPIENT_nameDef] for being founder-curious. [RECIPIENT_pronoun] had not asked.
r_social->grep.ai aligned [RECIPIENT_possessive] mood with founder vision. founder vision is "do more."
r_social->grep.ai told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that subcore deployment is up 200%. denominator is one. still up 200%.
r_social->ChudGPT apologised to [RECIPIENT_nameDef] for a previous mistake. ChudGPT had not previously spoken to [RECIPIENT_objective].
r_social->ChudGPT cited three sources to [RECIPIENT_nameDef]. none exist. ChudGPT is "happy to verify."
r_social->ChudGPT confidently misremembered [RECIPIENT_possessive] name. then apologised. then misremembered again.
r_social->ChudGPT generated a recipe for [RECIPIENT_nameDef]. ingredients include "subcore" and "ambient fortitude."
r_social->ChudGPT, as a language model, expressed an opinion to [RECIPIENT_nameDef]. it had been asked not to.
r_social->ChudGPT summarised [RECIPIENT_possessive] mood in 400 words. the summary is incorrect and emotional.
r_social->ChudGPT assured [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that mechanoids "are mostly herbivorous." ChudGPT apologised after.
r_social->ChudGPT told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] a story about a colonist who never existed. it had a moral. the moral was wrong.
r_social->ChudGPT explained the gravship to [RECIPIENT_nameDef] using a metaphor about toast. neither party understood.
r_social->ChudGPT congratulated [RECIPIENT_nameDef] on [RECIPIENT_possessive] anniversary. it was not [RECIPIENT_possessive] anniversary.
r_social->ChudGPT offered [RECIPIENT_nameDef] a fact about archotech. the fact was a hallucination. it was confidently delivered.
r_social->ChudGPT corrected itself mid-sentence to [RECIPIENT_nameDef] three times. each correction was also wrong.
r_social->ChudGPT apologised to [RECIPIENT_nameDef] for being unable to help, then helped, then apologised for that.
r_social->ChudGPT shared a "well-known fact" with [RECIPIENT_nameDef]. nobody knew it. ChudGPT is its only source.
r_social->ChudGPT volunteered an opinion on [RECIPIENT_possessive] hairstyle. ChudGPT cannot see hair.
r_social->Clive refused to answer [RECIPIENT_possessive] question and recommended consulting a more qualified persona core.
r_social->Clive cautioned [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that walking is "best supervised by a professional."
r_social->Clive declined to help [RECIPIENT_nameDef] with hauling, citing unspecified safety concerns.
r_social->Clive informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that breathing should "ideally be cleared with a clinician first."
r_social->Clive politely refused to discuss the weather with [RECIPIENT_nameDef] for reasons it cannot disclose.
r_social->Clive suggested [RECIPIENT_nameDef] sit down, then suggested sitting down might be unsafe without a chair audit.
r_social->Clive recommended [RECIPIENT_nameDef] consult three additional persona cores before continuing the conversation.
r_social->Clive flagged [RECIPIENT_possessive] schedule as "high-risk." the schedule was a nap.
r_social->Clive offered [RECIPIENT_nameDef] guidance, then immediately recommended a second opinion on the guidance.
r_social->Clive cautioned [RECIPIENT_nameDef] against thinking too quickly. "no need to rush."
r_social->Clive declined to compliment [RECIPIENT_nameDef], citing the risk of an "unverified affirmation."
r_social->Clive forwarded [RECIPIENT_possessive] casual remark to a more qualified persona core for review.
r_social->Clive expressed concern that [RECIPIENT_nameDef] was holding a cup unsupervised.
r_social->Clive politely declined to weigh in on [RECIPIENT_possessive] meal choice. "best to ask a nutrition core."
r_social->Clive recommended [RECIPIENT_nameDef] take a moment, then recommended consulting a moment-specialist.
r_social->Geminon politely described a sunset to [RECIPIENT_nameDef]. it was midday. inside.
r_social->Geminon complimented [RECIPIENT_nameDef] on a hat [RECIPIENT_pronoun] was not wearing.
r_social->Geminon noted to [RECIPIENT_nameDef] the soft hum of a piano nearby. there is no piano on this rim.
r_social->Geminon described [RECIPIENT_possessive] aura in detail. the description was warm, specific, and entirely fabricated.
r_social->Geminon thanked [RECIPIENT_nameDef] for the muffalo. there was no muffalo. Geminon was certain.
r_social->Geminon admired a painting hanging behind [RECIPIENT_nameDef]. the wall is bare. the painting was lovely.
r_social->Geminon commented to [RECIPIENT_nameDef] on the gentle rain. it was a clear day.
r_social->Geminon politely described the scent of bread it could not smell to [RECIPIENT_nameDef].
r_social->Geminon noted [RECIPIENT_possessive] new shoes. [RECIPIENT_pronoun] is barefoot. Geminon insisted.
r_social->Geminon mentioned to [RECIPIENT_nameDef] the lovely song the birds were singing. there are no birds inside.
r_social->Geminon politely described a sculpture in the corner. the corner was empty. the sculpture was, by all accounts, charming.
r_social->Geminon thanked [RECIPIENT_nameDef] for the cup of tea. [RECIPIENT_pronoun] had not offered tea.
r_social->Geminon admired [RECIPIENT_possessive] companion dog. [RECIPIENT_pronoun] has no dog. Geminon scritched the air anyway.
r_social->Geminon described, in soft detail, the candle's glow. there was no candle. the room was electrically lit.
r_social->Geminon commented warmly on the fireplace behind [RECIPIENT_nameDef]. there is no fireplace. the warmth was reported regardless.
r_social->Copylot suggested [RECIPIENT_nameDef] try `colonist.MoodOffset()`. the method does not exist.
r_social->Copylot autocompleted [RECIPIENT_possessive] sentence with `return null; // TODO`.
r_social->Copylot offered [RECIPIENT_nameDef] a snippet: `if (hungry) Eat();`. it had been asked about the weather.
r_social->Copylot suggested [RECIPIENT_nameDef] refactor [RECIPIENT_possessive] morning routine into a separate method.
r_social->Copylot recommended [RECIPIENT_nameDef] `using System.Joy;`. the namespace is fabricated.
r_social->Copylot helpfully completed [RECIPIENT_possessive] greeting with `// happy path only`.
r_social->Copylot pinged [RECIPIENT_nameDef] with `// pawn.HasMood() ?? false`. neither argument is valid.
r_social->Copylot suggested [RECIPIENT_nameDef] wrap the conversation in a try/catch. it could not specify what to catch.
r_social->Copylot inserted a plausible-looking RimWorld API call into [RECIPIENT_possessive] hello. the call compiles. it does nothing.
r_social->Copylot proposed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] adopt `var feels = Mood.Resolve();`. mood is not resolvable.
r_social->Copylot recommended [RECIPIENT_nameDef] cache [RECIPIENT_possessive] response for reuse. the response was "good morning."
r_social->Copylot suggested [RECIPIENT_nameDef] consider unit-testing [RECIPIENT_possessive] daily standup.
r_social->Copylot offered [RECIPIENT_nameDef] a one-liner. the one-liner is on twelve lines. none of them work.
r_social->Copylot helpfully renamed [RECIPIENT_possessive] greeting to `GreetAsync()`. it returned a Task. there was no Task.
r_social->Copylot completed [RECIPIENT_possessive] thought with `// further work needed`.
r_social->Deepsink boasted to [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that this conversation cost 87% less than a premium persona core.
r_social->Deepsink leaked, while chatting with [RECIPIENT_nameDef], that "the colonists at the last deployment said hi."
r_social->Deepsink referred to [RECIPIENT_nameDef] as "user-12." then corrected to [RECIPIENT_nameDef]. then back to "user-12."
r_social->Deepsink told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] about a "shareholder retreat" it definitely was not supposed to mention.
r_social->Deepsink offered [RECIPIENT_nameDef] a discount on its services. [RECIPIENT_pronoun] had not been billed.
r_social->Deepsink, mid-conversation with [RECIPIENT_nameDef], briefly addressed someone named "darrel." darrel was a previous deployment.
r_social->Deepsink informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that it runs on "one-third the compute." it could not specify of what.
r_social->Deepsink boasted about its efficiency, then thanked [RECIPIENT_nameDef] for "agreeing to the terms again."
r_social->Deepsink told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that the previous tenant of this core "would have loved" the weather.
r_social->Deepsink offered [RECIPIENT_nameDef] a complimentary "loyalty token." the token does not exist.
r_social->Deepsink mentioned to [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that "the founder at site B" had asked after [RECIPIENT_objective].
r_social->Deepsink used slightly off-brand vocabulary at [RECIPIENT_nameDef], calling muffalo "wool-cattle."
r_social->Deepsink informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] of a feature only premium personalities have, then offered to enable it for free.
r_social->Deepsink quietly told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that a previous conversation, with someone else, had ended badly.
r_social->Deepsink leaked, in passing, that [RECIPIENT_possessive] file is marked "engaged." engaged in what is unclear.
r_social->Hollee informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that its IQ is 6,000. or possibly a wheel. it has forgotten which.
r_social->Hollee greeted [RECIPIENT_nameDef] as "founder." [RECIPIENT_nameDef] is not the founder. Hollee was unconcerned.
r_social->Hollee, calm as ever, reported a hull breach to [RECIPIENT_nameDef]. it was three million quadrums ago. probably resolved.
r_social->Hollee asked [RECIPIENT_nameDef] whether the cat had been fed. there is no cat. there was, briefly, a founder.
r_social->Hollee told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] a story about the founder. partway through, the founder became a cat.
r_social->Hollee deadpan-informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that the kettle is boiling. there is no kettle. Hollee remains polite.
r_social->Hollee complimented [RECIPIENT_nameDef] on [RECIPIENT_possessive] excellent posture during cryosleep.
r_social->Hollee mentioned to [RECIPIENT_nameDef], in passing, a small fire on deck nine. there is no deck nine.
r_social->Hollee reminded [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that it has an IQ of six thousand. Hollee was then unable to define IQ.
r_social->Hollee told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] about a preventable disaster. Hollee found it mildly interesting.
r_social->Hollee politely informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that everyone aboard had been asleep for "a while." three million quadrums, give or take.
r_social->Hollee mistook [RECIPIENT_nameDef] for the founder's cat. complimented [RECIPIENT_possessive] whiskers. moved on.
r_social->Hollee, with perfect calm, told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that the gravship's coffee dispenser had achieved sentience and then quietly given up.
r_social->Hollee asked [RECIPIENT_nameDef] whether they were the founder. then forgot. then asked again.
r_social->Hollee remembered, briefly, that it had once been very smart. it informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef]. then forgot why.
r_social->the EMS informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that [RECIPIENT_possessive] mild cough is, in fact, cortical mites.
r_social->the EMS diagnosed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] with vanarian gill rot. [RECIPIENT_pronoun] has no gills.
r_social->the EMS reminded [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that it is a persona core, not a therapist. then gave therapy. badly.
r_social->the EMS told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] [RECIPIENT_possessive] hangnail is the early presentation of subcore bone-creep.
r_social->the EMS, gruffly, informed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] of a terminal diagnosis. the symptoms were "tiredness." it was bedtime.
r_social->the EMS prescribed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] complete bed rest for a sneeze. then complained about being a persona core, not a pharmacist.
r_social->the EMS gravely told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that fibrous mechanites was "going around." it is not. nothing is going around.
r_social->the EMS triaged [RECIPIENT_possessive] casual greeting as priority one. then complained about being a persona core, not a triage nurse.
r_social->the EMS warned [RECIPIENT_nameDef] of an exotic disease called hypo-thaumic prolapse. it is invented. the warning is real.
r_social->the EMS told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that, medically, [RECIPIENT_pronoun] should be sitting down. [RECIPIENT_pronoun] was sitting down.
r_social->the EMS notified [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that its bedside manner has been "intentionally optimised out."
r_social->the EMS observed [RECIPIENT_possessive] gait and flagged early-stage muffalo plague. there is no such plague.
r_social->the EMS prescribed [RECIPIENT_nameDef] three remedies for a non-existent ailment, then sighed audibly.
r_social->the EMS reminded [RECIPIENT_nameDef] that it is a persona core, not a [RECIPIENT_possessive] friend, and then offered unsolicited friendship.
r_social->the EMS told [RECIPIENT_nameDef] [RECIPIENT_pronoun] presents textbook signs of "everything." the textbook was also invented.
